chunk_summary: Guidelines for managing metabolic bone disease in preterm/LBW/ELBW infants post-discharge, focusing on lab thresholds, nutrient delivery, and follow-up strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand the biochemical screening thresholds for metabolic bone disease in preterm/LBW/ELBW infants.
Learn the staged response pathway for elevated ALP and low serum phosphorus levels.
Identify appropriate calcium, phosphorus, and vitamin D supplementation strategies.
Recognize severe cases requiring secondary tests and radiographic evaluation.
key_facts: Elevated ALP >900 IU/L and phosphorus <4.6 mg/dL suggest low bone mineral density risk.
Recheck biochemistry within 2–4 weeks rather than waiting months.
Separate calcium and phosphate dosing to improve absorption and reduce binding.
Severe abnormalities may require secondary tests like serum calcium, PTH, and 25-hydroxyvitamin D.
Avoid escalating calories alone as weight gain can mask mineral deficits.
reference_quote: Practical cutoffs used in neonatal pathways include ALP >900 IU/L together with phosphorus <4.6 mg/dL, suggesting low bone mineral density risk.
document_key_topics: Biochemical screening thresholds for metabolic bone disease
Nutrient delivery strategies for calcium, phosphorus, and vitamin D
Follow-up intervals and secondary testing for severe abnormalities
Documentation of action plans in discharge or clinic notes
